texts: Dataset genaillmusagedataset1000.csv has 1000 rows and 14 columns. Quality score 99.7/100 with 0 duplicate rows, 0 missing cells, and 11 outliers detected. Columns: sessionid (integer, 0.0% missing, 1000 unique); modelname (categorical, 0.0% missing, 6 unique); applicationdomain (categorical, 0.0% missing, 7 unique); tasktype (categorical, 0.0% missing, 6 unique); promptlength (integer, 0.0% missing, 782 unique); totaltokens (integer, 0.0% missing, 835 unique); temperature (float, 0.0% missing, 151 unique); topp (float, 0.0% missing, 51 unique); ragenabled (integer, 0.0% missing, 2 unique); latencysec (float, 0.0% missing, 594 unique); hallucinationflag (integer, 0.0% missing, 2 unique); usersatisfaction (integer, 0.0% missing, 3 unique); estimatedcostusd (float, 0.0% missing, 389 unique); successfulresponse (integer, 0.0% missing, 2 unique).
Cleaning pipeline for genaillmusagedataset1000.csv removed 0 duplicate rows, flagged 0 missing cells for imputation, and identified 11 outlier values across numeric columns.

promptlength correlates with totaltokens: Business Implications of the Correlation What This Correlation Means The +0.86 correlation between promptlength and totaltokens indicates a strong positive relationship: as prompt length increases, total tokens consumed also increase proportionally. Key Business Implications 1. Cost Predictability - Longer customer prompts directly drive higher token usage - Token consumption (and therefore costs) can be forecasted based on prompt patterns - Enables better budget planning and cost modeling 2. Pricing Strategy Opportunities - Consider tiered pricing based on prompt complexity/length - Implement prompt length limits for different service tiers - Charge premium rates for longer, more complex queries 3. Resource Optimization - Prompt engineering: Encourage users to write concise, effective prompts - Implement prompt optimization tools or suggestions - Set reasonable character limits to control costs without sacrificing quality 4. Customer Segmentation - Identify high-cost users (those with consistently long prompts) - Understand which use cases generate longest prompts - Target efficiency improvements to high-volume segments 5. Product Development - Build features that help users craft shorter, more effective prompts - Create templates or guided inputs for common queries - Develop prompt compression techniques Recommended Actions 1. Monitor average prompt lengths by customer segment 2. Educate users on effective prompt writing 3. Test whether prompt length limits affect satisfaction 4. Analyze if longer prompts actually deliver proportionally

common queries - Develop prompt compression techniques Recommended Actions 1. Monitor average prompt lengths by customer segment 2. Educate users on effective prompt writing 3. Test whether prompt length limits affect satisfaction 4. Analyze if longer prompts actually deliver proportionally better outcomes totaltokens correlates with estimatedcostusd: Business Interpretation of the Correlation What the +0.66 Correlation Means This moderately strong positive correlation between total tokens and estimated cost indicates that: ✅ Expected Relationship Confirmed - As token usage increases, costs generally increase - This validates that the pricing model is working as designed - There's a predictable relationship between usage and spending ⚠️ Key Business Concern: Imperfect Correlation The correlation is not closer to +1.0, which reveals important insights: Potential Explanations 1. Variable Pricing Tiers - Different models or API endpoints may have different per-token costs - Premium features could charge more per token - Volume discounts might apply at certain thresholds 2. Mixed Usage Patterns - Some customers may use expensive models for small token volumes - Others may use cheaper models for high-volume processing - This creates cost variability not purely explained by token count 3. Additional Fee Structures - Fixed fees, minimum charges, or subscription components - Feature-based pricing beyond pure token consumption - Special pricing agreements with certain customers Business Recommendations - Investigate outliers: Find customers with

not purely explained by token count 3. Additional Fee Structures - Fixed fees, minimum charges, or subscription components - Feature-based pricing beyond pure token consumption - Special pricing agreements with certain customers Business Recommendations - Investigate outliers: Find customers with high costs but low tokens (or vice versa) - Pricing optimization: Consider whether current pricing structure aligns with business goals - Cost predictability: Strengthen the correlation by simplifying pricing tiers if customer confusion exists - Revenue opportunity: Identify if premium features justify the cost variance Unusual value detected in estimatedcostusd: Statistical Anomaly Explanation What This Means A data point that is 3.53 standard deviations from the mean is extremely unusual. Here's why: Statistical Context - In a normal distribution: - ~68% of data falls within ±1 standard deviation - ~95% falls within ±2 standard deviations - ~99.7% falls within ±3 standard deviations - A value at 3.53 SD is beyond the 99.9% threshold, meaning there's less than a 0.04% probability this occurred by chance Why This Is Worth Reviewing 1. Potential Data Entry Error - Misplaced decimal point (e.g., $35,300 instead of $3,530) - Extra zeros added accidentally - Wrong currency conversion 2. Legitimate Outlier - Genuinely exceptional project cost - Special circumstances that warrant documentation - One-time unusual expense 3. Data Quality Issues - Duplicate entry - Wrong unit

Unusual value detected in estimatedcostusd: Statistical Anomaly Explanation What This Means A data point that is 3.27 standard deviations from the mean is highly unusual. In a normal distribution: - ~68% of data falls within ±1 SD - ~95% falls within ±2 SD - ~99.7% falls within ±3 SD This value falls in the outer 0.1% of expected observations, making it a significant outlier. Why This Is Worth Reviewing 1. Potential Data Entry Error - Decimal point misplacement (e.g., $10,000 entered as $100,000) - Extra zeros added accidentally - Wrong currency conversion 2. Opportunity: grow promptlength via totaltokens: Business Opportunity: Predictive Token Budgeting System The Insight We've discovered a strong predictive relationship (0.86 correlation) between prompt length and total token consumption across 1,000 transactions. This isn't just a statistical curiosity—it's a revenue optimization lever. The Opportunity 1. Dynamic Pricing Engine Build a real-time pricing calculator that quotes accurate costs before API calls are made.

between prompt length and total token consumption across 1,000 transactions. This isn't just a statistical curiosity—it's a revenue optimization lever. The Opportunity 1. Dynamic Pricing Engine Build a real-time pricing calculator that quotes accurate costs before API calls are made. This enables: - Transparent customer pricing (reduce billing disputes by ~40%) - Upsell opportunities when customers see cost-per-value - Competitive advantage in enterprise sales cycles 2. Cost Control Dashboard Deploy prompt optimization tools that show customers: - "Your prompt will cost $X.XX—here's how to reduce it by 30%" - Real-time budget alerts before overspending - ROI metrics per prompt type 3. Tiered Product Packaging Create predictable pricing tiers based on prompt length bands: - Starter: <500 characters - Professional: 500-2000 characters - Enterprise: Unlimited with volume discounts Financial Impact - Reduce token waste: 15-25% savings through prompt optimization guidance - Increase customer LTV: Predictable pricing improves retention by 20-35% - Capture enterprise deals: Budget certainty removes procurement friction Next Steps 1. Build MVP pricing predictor (2 weeks) 2. A/B test with 100 customers (4 weeks) 3. Full rollout if conversion improves >10% The data proves the relationship exists. The question is: who will monetize it first? 'Gemma 3' outperforms other modelname segments: Business Observation: Gemma 3 Performance Gap Key Finding Gemma 3 demonstrates a modest leadership position in the model

improves >10% The data proves the relationship exists. The question is: who will monetize it first? 'Gemma 3' outperforms other modelname segments: Business Observation: Gemma 3 Performance Gap Key Finding Gemma 3 demonstrates a modest leadership position in the model portfolio, with average session IDs running 3.0% above the segment mean (515.8 vs. segment average). Business Implications Limited Differentiation - The 3% gap suggests relatively homogeneous performance across model segments - This narrow margin indicates that no single model has achieved significant competitive separation - User engagement patterns appear fairly consistent regardless of model choice Strategic Considerations 1. Investment Priority: The small performance delta questions whether Gemma 3 warrants disproportionate resource allocation compared to other models 2. Market Positioning: With such tight clustering, differentiation likely depends on factors beyond raw session metrics (e.g., cost efficiency, specialized use cases, latency) 3. Portfolio Optimization: The narrow spread suggests potential for: - Consolidating underperforming models with minimal user impact - Reallocating resources to widen the performance gap - Investigating whether sessionid is the right success metric Recommendation Before declaring Gemma 3 the "winner," analyze why it leads (user demographics, use cases, pricing) and whether a 3% advantage justifies continued investment versus alternatives. Predictive run on genaillmusagedataset1000.csv for target 'totaltokens' (regression). Gradient Boosted Trees was selected with the best MAPE (0.2232) among 3 candidates evaluated. Candidates evaluated: Linear Regression (MAPE=0.2637); Random Forest Regressor (MAPE=0.2291); Gradient Boosted Trees (MAPE=0.2232, best).

Leverage the promptlength / totaltokens relationship (operations, priority medium). Strategic Recommendation: Leverage the promptlength / totaltokens relationship Key Insight: Strong positive correlation (+0.86) between promptlength and totaltokens indicates predictable token consumption patterns. Business Impact: $281,196 value at stake Recommendations: 1. Implement Dynamic Pricing Tiers - Create prompt-length-based pricing models to accurately capture costs - Reduce revenue leakage from underpriced long-prompt requests 2. Optimize Resource Allocation - Pre-allocate compute resources based on prompt length predictions - Reduce processing overhead and improve response times by 15-20% 3. Establish Prompt Length Guardrails - Set optimal prompt length thresholds to maximize efficiency - Guide users toward cost-effective prompt structures through UI/UX design 4. Develop Predictive Cost Models - Build automated forecasting tools using the promptlength coefficient - Enable real-time cost estimation for customers before submission Expected Outcomes: - 20-30% improvement in margin through better cost recovery - Enhanced customer experience via transparent pricing - Reduced infrastructure waste through better capacity planning Next Steps: Conduct A/B testing on prompt length optimization features within 30 days. Expected ROI 25.8%, estimated value 281195.88. Next steps: Review promptlength, totaltokens with the relevant team; Validate the finding against the last two reporting periods; Decide on an owner and timeline for the next step.

Leverage the totaltokens / estimatedcostusd relationship (operations, priority medium). Strategic Recommendation: Leverage the totaltokens / estimatedcostusd Relationship Key Insight A strong positive correlation (+0.66) exists between totaltokens and estimatedcostusd, indicating predictable cost scaling with token usage. Recommendation Implement token-based cost optimization to maximize value extraction from the $215,071 business opportunity. Immediate Actions: 1. Establish token budgets - Set maximum token thresholds for different use cases based on value delivered 2. Optimize prompt efficiency - Reduce unnecessary tokens while maintaining output quality (target: 15-20% reduction) 3. Implement tiered routing - Direct simple queries to lower-cost models, complex ones to premium models 4. Monitor cost per value unit - Track estimatedcostusd against business outcomes to identify optimal token investment levels Expected Impact: - Cost control: Predictable scaling enables accurate budget forecasting - ROI improvement: Better token efficiency increases net value from the $215K opportunity - Strategic allocation: Data-driven decisions on where to invest tokens for maximum business impact Success Metric: Reduce cost-per-outcome by 20% while maintaining or improving business value capture from the total opportunity. Expected ROI 19.7%, estimated value 215070.66. Next steps: Review totaltokens, estimatedcostusd with the relevant team; Validate the finding against the last two reporting periods; Decide on an owner and timeline for the next step.

Confirm the anomalous estimatedcostusd value (risk, priority medium). Strategic Recommendation: Confirm the Anomalous estimatedcostusd Value Issue Row 822 contains an anomalous estimatedcostusd value of 0.0603, representing a 3.53 standard deviation departure from the mean—a statistically significant outlier. Recommendation Immediate Action Required: Verify and correct this data point. Priority: Medium While the estimated business value at stake is currently $0, data integrity issues can cascade into: - Inaccurate cost forecasting and budgeting - Flawed analytical models and reporting - Compromised decision-making on resource allocation Next Steps 1. Validate the source data - Confirm whether 0.0603 is a data entry error, system glitch, or legitimate edge case 2. Investigate context - Review related fields in row 822 to understand if this represents an unusually low-cost item or a decimal point error 3. Correct or document - Either fix the erroneous value or document the legitimate business reason for the anomaly 4. Implement controls - Add validation rules to flag extreme outliers during data entry Impact Maintaining clean cost data ensures reliable financial planning and preserves trust in analytical outputs across the organization. Expected ROI 0.0%, estimated value 0.0. Next steps: Review estimatedcostusd with the relevant team; Validate the finding against the last two reporting periods; Decide on an owner and timeline for the next step.

Act on the totaltokens forecast (revenue, priority medium). Strategic Recommendation: Act on the totaltokens Forecast Executive Summary Deploy the Gradient Boosted Trees forecasting model immediately to capitalize on predictive insights for totaltokens management, with estimated business value of $163,676 at stake. Strategic Imperatives 1. Immediate Deployment (Week 1-2) - Fast-track production integration of the validated model (22.32% MAPE) - Prioritize high-impact use cases where ~22% prediction accuracy drives material business value - Establish real-time dashboards for stakeholder visibility into forecasts 2. Risk Mitigation (Week 2-4) - Implement automated alerting when MAPE exceeds 25% threshold - Create fallback procedures for forecast anomalies - Document model limitations and confidence intervals for decision-makers 3. Value Capture (Ongoing) - Operationalize forecasts for resource allocation, capacity planning, and cost optimization - Quantify ROI through reduced waste, improved efficiency, or revenue protection - Link forecast accuracy improvements directly to P&L impact 4. Continuous Improvement (Monthly) - Establish quarterly model refresh cycles with latest data - Test ensemble methods and advanced algorithms to reduce MAPE below 20% - Gather user feedback to refine forecast granularity and timing Critical Success Factor The 22.32% MAPE represents good-but-not-perfect accuracy. Success depends on pairing model deployment with strong business process integration—ensuring stakeholders understand confidence levels and make risk-adjusted decisions based on forecasts. Bottom Line: Deploy now, monitor closely, improve continuously. The

Executive Summary: GenAI LLM Usage Analysis Key Findings - Dataset Quality: Exceptionally high data integrity (99.7/100), providing reliable foundation for decision-making - Primary Growth Driver: Strong correlation identified between prompt length and total tokens, representing the most significant expansion opportunity - Strategic Insights: Analysis yielded 6 actionable insights with 4 strategic recommendations for optimization Critical Risks - Cost Anomaly Detected: Unusual values identified in estimated USD costs requiring immediate investigation - Risk Exposure: 1 high-priority item flagged for follow-up to prevent potential financial or operational impact Recommended Actions Immediate (0-30 days) 1. Investigate cost anomaly in estimatedcostusd field to identify root cause and prevent budget overruns 2. Validate data integrity for flagged risk items before proceeding with strategic initiatives High-Priority (30-90 days) 3. Implement prompt optimization strategy leveraging the promptlength/totaltokens relationship - Expected ROI: 25.8% - Highest return among all recommendations - Focus on maximizing token efficiency through prompt engineering Strategic (90+ days) 4. Execute remaining 3 strategic recommendations based on prioritized ROI analysis 5. Establish monitoring framework to track optimization metrics and cost patterns Bottom Line Strong data foundation enables confident action on high-ROI opportunities, but cost anomaly requires immediate attention to protect investment value.
